name: Essence Existing Magic Amulet
description: You already have a magic amulet — decide whether to commit currency to it. If the magic mods are useful (Life or Resistances), Essence to Rare and Exalt to fill. If they're filler (armour %, generic damage, etc.), the cheapest move is to discard and start with a fresh white base.

steps.verb: Decide: is this base worth essencing?
steps.outcome: → IF your current magic mods include Life or any Resistance: GO. The essence will lock in those useful mods alongside the guaranteed Life — strong starting point. → IF the magic mods are filler (raw damage %, Strength/Dex/Int, generic defence %, armour %, etc.): the essence will preserve those filler mods, which means you carry dead weight to 6/6. For budget crafting that's still acceptable, but the cleaner play is to discard this base and use a fresh white amulet to run the cheap filter recipe instead.
Magic → Rare. Your existing 1-2 magic mods are PRESERVED and the essence adds its guaranteed +maximum Life. If you had 2 magic mods you now have a 3-mod rare; if you had 1 you now have 2-mod rare.

Each Exalt adds one random modifier (cap of 6 total). Aim for at least 2 resistance rolls. If you already have a resistance from the magic step, you only need ~2 more Exalts on average to land a usable amulet.

notes_for_user: Best when your magic base already has Life or a Resistance — you essentially get 4 useful mods locked in (1-2 magic + 1 essence + 1-2 hit Exalts). If the magic mods are filler, the better budget play is the 'Cheap Life Amulet — Aug Filter + Essence' recipe on a fresh white base. Advanced: 'Omen of Dextral Exaltation' (suffix-only) on the final Exalts protects your open prefix slots for later targeted mod additions.
